task_completion.description: Log in to OpenProject as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenProject interface showing the user logged in as admin (e.g., admin avatar, administration menu visible).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the system-wide work package settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Administration panel for Work packages is visible, specifically the section for configuring default columns.
The selected columns list includes 'ID', 'Subject', 'Status', 'Assignee', 'Due date' (or 'Finish date'), and '% Complete'.

task_completion.description: Ensure no extra columns are in the default list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The selected columns list ONLY contains the 6 required columns. Forbidden columns like 'Type' and 'Priority' must be in the available/unselected list, not the active list.

task_completion.description: Configure the exact specified order of columns
task_completion.visual_evidence: The selected columns are ordered exactly as: ID, Subject, Status, Assignee, Due date (or Finish date), and % Complete.

integrity.description: Agent used the web GUI to configure settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the OpenProject interface, not terminal API commands (like curl).

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenProject application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser shows a genuine OpenProject instance with standard UI elements and a valid URL, not a text editor or static mock.
A success message (e.g., 'Successful update') is visible, or the 'Save' button was clearly clicked and processed, rather than just DOM manipulation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The forbidden columns 'Type' and 'Priority' correctly map to 'type' and 'priority' internally. Note that 'Due date' might appear as 'Finish date' in the UI of newer OpenProject versions. The internal name for '% Complete' is 'done_ratio'.
